classification.affected_component: CIBA backchannel authentication ping mode — BackchannelAuthenticationCallbackEndpoint

signals.confirmation_signal: Keycloak sends unconditional outbound HTTP POST to attacker-configured notification endpoint (127.0.0.1:9999); Docker logs confirm TCP connection attempt
signals.root_cause: UriUtils.checkUrl() validates only URL scheme, not hostname. No loopback/RFC-1918 blocking on notification_endpoint registration.

retrieval_summary.summary: Blind SSRF in CIBA ping mode: attacker registers a client with a notification_endpoint pointing to an internal address (127.x, RFC-1918, link-local). Keycloak performs unconditional outbound HTTP POST with no hostname validation. Docker evidence: connection attempt to http://127.0.0.1:9999/notify (failed only due to ECONNREFUSED, not validation). Requires admin access or valid Initial Access Token.
retrieval_summary.fix_recommendation: Reject loopback (127.x/::1), link-local (169.254.x), and RFC-1918 ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x) in UriUtils.checkUrl()
